summary: Review-response.html enumerates five strawman labels against our critique; none identifies a genuine weakness in our existing prose. Four of five labels attack positions we do not hold (we already credit dome's honest caveats, attribute standard-model mechanisms, and reserve absolute language for structural arguments). In at least two cases (WIN-013/014 reclassified supportive_nonunique; WIN-058 reclassified pending_contested) the dome's response-page concessions absorb the exact critique our review made while branding it a strawman. Recommend closing ISS-1073; EXP-171.json contains the label-by-label analysis. Three optional follow-up additions are listed (review-response acknowledgement section, WIN-058 API disambiguation note, TLDR absolute-language calibration pass) but none are blocking. Canary remains at partial-rhetorical-adoption — structural predictions-only OTS anchor still unimplemented.
newsworthy: Dome author publishes 'Strawman Control' page branding our critique patterns as strawmen — while the same API release reclassifies WIN-013/014 and WIN-058 into exactly the non-discriminating/pending buckets we said they belonged in. Strawman label and concession arrive in the same commit.
